weather.tip: Mostly clear skies but a 32% chance of showers later tonight — worth keeping a light rain jacket or umbrella in the car or bag rather than betting on the clear evening holding.

around.headline: Third Bengaluru school-linked death this week: Class 8 girl dies by suicide after alleged humiliation
around.summary: A Class 8 student died by suicide, leaving a note seeking forgiveness from her family after alleging she was humiliated at school — following this week's other two school-linked deaths (a child crushed by a school van, and a boy who died during a drill). If your child seems withdrawn about school, it's worth checking in directly rather than waiting for them to raise it.

around.headline: Namma Metro's Green Line disrupted twice today, including a glitch at Yelachenahalli station
around.summary: BMRCL confirmed a technical glitch on the Green Line during Thursday's morning peak, with a separate issue reported later at Yelachenahalli station. If you rely on the Green Line for tomorrow's commute, build in extra buffer time in case disruptions continue.

around.headline: Reminder: GBA starts towing abandoned vehicles from tomorrow, July 10
around.summary: Greater Bengaluru Authority's five city corporations begin clearing vehicles abandoned on roads and footpaths from tomorrow; South Bengaluru alone has identified seven lots to hold towed vehicles. If your car or bike has been parked long-term on a public street, move it tonight.
around.source: The New Indian Express / Public TV English

around.headline: Karnataka Lokayukta's mega crackdown: 53 raids expose assets worth nearly ₹50 crore
around.summary: The Lokayukta conducted simultaneous raids at 53 locations tied to 10 officials, uncovering assets worth close to ₹50 crore. A reminder the anti-corruption body is actively pursuing large disproportionate-assets cases right now.

around.headline: Karnataka settles 8.82 lakh pending traffic e-challans, collects ₹26.37 crore
around.summary: The state government cleared a backlog of 8.82 lakh pending e-challans, collecting ₹26.37 crore in fines in the process. Worth checking your own e-challan status online and clearing anything outstanding before it piles up.

around.headline: Iran says it struck US targets in the Gulf; Trump claims he's Iran's 'No. 1 target'
around.summary: Iran reported hitting US military targets in the Gulf and is preparing to bury its slain Supreme Leader in Mashhad, while Trump said he believes he's Iran's top target — the standoff shows no sign of cooling. If it drags on, keep an eye on fuel prices when budgeting for travel.

events.name: Bengaluru International Short Film Festival (BISFF)
events.note: Citywide screenings; communications mandate and lineup details were confirmed this week.

events.name: Bengaluru Blasters vs Coastal Kings Mangaluru
events.note: Local T20 cricket fixture happening tomorrow.

events.name: Sankara Eye Hospitals & VISION 2020 India eye-care conference
events.when: Announced this week, exact dates awaited
events.note: Public eye-care conference in Bengaluru; watch for the schedule.

street_smart.title: A car cuts you off, you honk, and now they're chasing you down the expressway. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real cases this week: three people were arrested for chasing and assaulting a family in a road-rage incident on the Bengaluru-Mysuru Expressway, and a separate family was attacked near Maddur on the same highway before three attackers were held.
street_smart.situation: You're driving on the Bengaluru-Mysuru Expressway with your family. A car swerves in front of you; you honk. Now it's matching your speed alongside you, and the driver is gesturing at you to pull over. Your kids are in the back seat. What do you do?

choices.text: Speed up and try to lose them, weaving through traffic to break away.
choices.feedback: Trying to outrun an aggressor at highway speed raises your own crash risk and can escalate the chase rather than end it. Getting to a safe, public place matters more than winning a speed contest.

choices.text: Slow down, roll your window down, and try to reason or apologize to calm them down.
choices.feedback: This is close to how the families on the Mysuru Expressway ended up assaulted — stopping or opening your window for an aggressor invites them to escalate to physical violence instead of defusing anything. Never negotiate face-to-face with a road-rage driver.

choices.text: Don't stop or engage — keep windows up and doors locked, glance at their number plate only if it's safe to do so, and drive to the nearest toll plaza, police checkpoint, or well-lit petrol bunk. Call 100 or 112 once you're in a public, safe spot.
choices.feedback: Correct. A toll plaza, police post, or busy petrol bunk has people and often CCTV, which de-escalates most aggressors and gives police something to act on. Karnataka Police have made arrests within days in these highway road-rage cases using vehicle numbers and footage.

choices.text: Ignore it and keep driving at the same speed, assuming they'll eventually back off on their own.
choices.feedback: This week's cases show attackers on this stretch didn't back off on their own — they pursued and physically assaulted the other vehicle's occupants. Passive driving without heading toward a safe, public location isn't enough.

takeaways: Never stop or roll down your windows for an aggressive driver on a highway — head toward the nearest public, well-lit, or CCTV-covered spot (toll plaza, police post, petrol bunk) before engaging at all.
Call 100 or 112 while en route if you're being followed; only note the vehicle's number plate if you can do so without slowing down or taking your eyes off the road for long.
If your vehicle is deliberately blocked or hit, phone or dashcam footage matters — it's what let police make arrests within days in this week's Mysuru Expressway cases.

quiz.q: Which Namma Metro line saw disruptions during Thursday's morning peak and a separate glitch at Yelachenahalli station?
quiz.why: BMRCL confirmed a technical glitch on the Green Line; Yelachenahalli is one of its southern stretch stations.

quiz.q: Karnataka's Lokayukta carried out simultaneous raids at how many locations in this week's crackdown, uncovering assets worth nearly ₹50 crore?
quiz.why: The raids targeted 10 officials in all, making it one of the Lokayukta's larger single-day operations this year.

quiz.q: According to reports this week, where is Iran laying its slain Supreme Leader to rest?
quiz.why: Fighter jets were reported escorting the coffin home for burial in Mashhad, a major Shia pilgrimage city in northeastern Iran.

quiz.q: Under India's Constitution, 'Police and Public Order' — the subject that lets Karnataka Police investigate cases like this week's expressway road-rage arrests — falls under which list?
quiz.why: Because policing is a State List subject, each state controls its own police force, which is why highway patrol response on the Mysuru Expressway is a Karnataka Police matter, not a central one.

quiz.q: Bengaluru's relatively mild year-round climate owes a lot to its elevation of roughly 900 metres. Which plateau is the city built on?
quiz.why: The Deccan Plateau's elevation is also why Bengaluru's evenings cool off faster than coastal cities at the same latitude.
